concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory used to store elements of the same data type.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A way of organizing and storing data in a computer so that it can be accessed and modified efficiently.

concepts.name: Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: A logical description of data and a set of operations that can be performed on that data, independent of its implementation.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: LIFO Principle
concepts.short_definition: The last element added to the structure is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Push Operation
concepts.short_definition: Adds an element to the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Pop Operation
concepts.short_definition: Removes and returns the element from the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Peek Operation
concepts.short_definition: Returns the element at the top of the stack without removing it.

concepts.name: IsEmpty Operation
concepts.short_definition: Checks if the stack is empty.

concepts.name: IsFull Operation
concepts.short_definition: Checks if the stack is full (relevant for fixed-size arrays).

concepts.name: Stack Overflow
concepts.short_definition: An error condition that occurs when a push operation is attempted on a full stack.

concepts.name: Stack Underflow
concepts.short_definition: An error condition that occurs when a pop or peek operation is attempted on an empty stack.

concepts.name: Top Pointer
concepts.short_definition: An index that points to the topmost element of the stack in an array implementation.

concepts.name: Fixed-Size Array
concepts.short_definition: An array whose size is determined at compile time and cannot be changed during runtime.

concepts.name: Dynamic Array
concepts.short_definition: An array that can grow or shrink in size during runtime.

concepts.name: Array Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing the stack ADT using an underlying array data structure.

concepts.name: Initialization
concepts.short_definition: Setting up the initial state of the stack, including allocating memory and setting the top pointer.

concepts.name: Capacity
concepts.short_definition: The maximum number of elements an array-based stack can hold.

concepts.name: Element Type
concepts.short_definition: The type of data that can be stored in the stack (e.g., integers, strings, objects).

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the time required to execute an algorithm as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Constant Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time does not depend on the size of the input.

concepts.name: Linear Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time grows linearly with the size of the input.

concepts.name: Push Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of the push operation in an array-based stack.

concepts.name: Pop Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of the pop operation in an array-based stack.

concepts.name: Peek Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of the peek operation in an array-based stack.

concepts.name: IsEmpty Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of the isEmpty operation in an array-based stack.

concepts.name: IsFull Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of the isFull operation in an array-based stack.

concepts.name: Space Complexity (Array Stack)
concepts.short_definition: The space complexity of an array-based stack implementation.

concepts.name: Array Indexing
concepts.short_definition: Accessing elements in an array using their numerical index.

concepts.name: Index Out of Bounds
concepts.short_definition: An error that occurs when trying to access an array element at an invalid index.

concepts.name: Zero-Based Indexing
concepts.short_definition: The first element of an array is at index 0.

concepts.name: Stack Applications
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems and algorithms where stacks are effectively used.

concepts.name: Function Call Stack
concepts.short_definition: A mechanism used by programming languages to manage function calls and their local variables.

concepts.name: Expression Evaluation
concepts.short_definition: Using stacks to convert and evaluate mathematical expressions.

concepts.name: Backtracking Algorithms
concepts.short_definition: Algorithms that explore possible solutions by trying to build a solution incrementally and abandoning paths that do not lead to a solution.

concepts.name: Undo/Redo Functionality
concepts.short_definition: Implementing undo and redo features in applications using stacks.

concepts.name: Memory Management
concepts.short_definition: How stacks are used in managing memory for program execution.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself.
